articleTitle: ReqFusion: A Multi-Provider Framework for Automated PEGS Analysis Across Software Domains
explanation:
Remember when Naruto struggled with the Rasengan because he tried to learn it all at once, but Kakashi taught him to break it down into rotation, power, and containment? That's exactly the breakthrough ReqFusion achieves in automated requirements engineering! Just like how the Fourth Hokage's sealed jutsu gave Naruto specific structural guidance to control the Nine-Tails' chakra, this paper tackles the notoriously labor-intensive challenge of software requirements extraction by using the structured PEGS framework (Project, Environment, Goals, System) to give Large Language Models explicit semantic anchors. Instead of asking LLMs to extract requirements with generic prompting - which is like asking Naruto to "just figure out" chakra control - ReqFusion decomposes the complex extraction task into four well-defined PEGS dimensions, transforming a single overwhelming prompt into focused sub-tasks with clear semantic boundaries.

The technical architecture mirrors the Hidden Leaf Village's multi-team coordination during the Fourth Great Ninja War - ReqFusion integrates three different LLM providers (OpenAI GPT, Anthropic Claude, and Groq) in a consensus-based system that acts like runtime verification. Just as different ninja squads cross-verified intelligence to prevent enemy deception jutsu, the multi-provider consensus mechanism reduces AI hallucination rates through cross-model agreement and provides fallback mechanisms when one model fails. The system processes multiple document formats (PDF, DOCX, PPTX) across academic, industrial, and tender proposal contexts, using domain-independent extraction methods that work like universal translation jutsu - adapting to different software domains without requiring specialized training for each context.

The empirical results are as impressive as Naruto's final Rasengan mastery! The ablation study reveals that PEGS-guided prompting achieves an F1 score of 0.88 compared to 0.71 for generic prompting under identical multi-provider configurations - that's a 24% improvement in extraction accuracy, proving that structured guidance dramatically outperforms brute-force approaches. The evaluation across 18 real-world documents generated 226 requirements with precise classification (54.9% functional, 45.1% non-functional), while extended testing on five projects with 1,050 requirements demonstrated a staggering 78% reduction in analysis time compared to manual methods. This isn't just about speed - it's about transforming requirements engineering from a chakra-draining manual process into an automated system that maintains both accuracy and comprehensive coverage across all requirement categories.

Like how Naruto's multi-shadow clone technique multiplied his training effectiveness while maintaining the original's knowledge, ReqFusion's multi-provider architecture enhances reliability through model consensus while the PEGS-based approach ensures no requirement category gets overlooked. The framework addresses critical gaps in AI-assisted software engineering by combining natural language processing automation with verification techniques - proving that just as Naruto needed both raw power and precise control to master his techniques, modern software development needs both AI efficiency and structured methodological rigor. This research doesn't just make requirements engineering faster; it makes it more reliable, comprehensive, and scalable across diverse software domains - a true step toward the AI-enhanced development workflows that will define the future of software engineering, dattebayo!
